Reference to Ronald Reagan Building and International Trade Center, , , provided that:Pub. L. 104–68, § 2109 Stat. 766Dec. 22, 1995“Any reference in a law, map, regulation, document, paper, or other record of the United States to the building referred to in section 1 [now ] shall be deemed to be a reference to the ‘Ronald Reagan Building and International Trade Center’.”40 U.S.C. 6734(a)

children.heading: .—Establishment and Designation
children.label: a .—Establishment and Designation
children.text: The building constructed on the Federal Triangle property shall be known and designated as the Ronald Reagan Building and International Trade Center.

children.heading: .—Title
children.label: b .—Title
children.text: The person selected to develop the Federal Triangle property may own the Building for not more than 35 years from the date construction of the Building began. The title to the Building shall be in the Administrator of General Services from the date title to the Federal Triangle property reverts to the Administrator.

children.heading: .—Size of building
children.label: 1 .—Size of building
children.text: The Building (including parking facilities) may not exceed 3,100,000 gross square feet in size.

children.heading: .—Height of building
children.label: 2 .—Height of building
children.text: The height of the Building shall be compatible with the height of surrounding Federal Government buildings.

children.label: A
children.text: be designed in harmony with historical and Government buildings in the vicinity;

children.label: B
children.text: reflect the symbolic importance and historic character of Pennsylvania Avenue and the Nation’s Capital; and

children.label: C
children.text: represent the dignity and stability of the Government.

children.heading: .—Construction Standards
children.label: d .—Construction Standards
children.text: The Building shall meet all standards applicable to construction of a federal building.

children.heading: .—Accounting System
children.label: e .—Accounting System
children.text: The Administrator shall maintain an accounting system for operation and maintenance of the Building which will allow accurate projections of the dates and cost of major repairs, improvements, reconstructions, and replacements of the Building and other capital expenditures on the Building. The Administrator shall act as necessary to ensure that amounts are available to cover the projected cost and expenditures.

children.heading: .—Lease agreement
children.label: 1 .—Lease agreement
children.text: Under an agreement with the person selected to construct the Ronald Reagan Building and International Trade Center, the Administrator shall lease the Building for federal office space and the international cultural and trade center space.

children.heading: .—Limit on length of lease
children.label: A .—Limit on length of lease
children.text: The Administrator will lease the Building for the period of time that the person selected to construct the Building owns the Building.

children.heading: .—Rental rate
children.label: B .—Rental rate
children.text: The rental rate per square foot of occupiable space for all space in the Building will be in the best interest of the Government and will carry out the objectives of this subchapter and the Federal Triangle Development Act (, ). The aggregate rental rate for all space in the Building shall produce an amount at least equal to the amount necessary to amortize the cost of development of the Federal Triangle property over the life of the lease.Public Law 100–113101 Stat. 735

children.heading: .—Obligation of amounts
children.label: C .—Obligation of amounts
children.text: Obligation of amounts from the Federal Building Fund shall only be made on an annual basis to meet lease payments.

children.heading: .—Authorization to obligate amounts
children.label: 3 .—Authorization to obligate amounts
children.text: Amounts may be obligated as described in paragraph (2)(C).
